title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
During his conversation with Jane, Poirot offers his assistance and mentions his connection to Edward Catchpole, a detective with the Metropolitan Police. This causes Jane to become highly distressed. She criticizes Poirot for being a foreigner and a retired policeman, then begs him to swear that if she dies, he will instruct his police friend not to investigate the killer or solve the case. Before Poirot can respond to her plea, Jane flees into the night.

Troubled by her sudden disappearance and feeling a sense of responsibility, Poirot finds he has no appetite for his dinner. He approaches the kitchen staff to seek information. A woman named Phyllis Sprung, who identifies herself as Euphemia, tells Poirot that Jane is "beyond help." After Poirot introduces himself as a Belgian, Phyllis agrees to answer his questions. 

Phyllis reveals that Jane previously worked as a maid for a wealthy family. She notes that Jane used to visit the cafe to purchase coffee and cake before her mistress's parties, often speaking in a manner that mimicked a lady. Poirot observes that Jane's voice had seemed fake and unnatural for a maid; Phyllis admits she cannot confirm the authenticity of the voice but acknowledges that Jane always spoke as if she were of noble standing.
